sections.paragraph:
to be fat, to become fat, Deu 31:20. (Arab. دَسِمَ id., ם and ן being interchanged.)

Piel-

(1) to make any thing fat, marrowy. Pro 15:30, שְׁמוּעָה טֹובָה תְּדַשֶּׁן־עָֽצֶם “good tidings make the bones fat,” as if, fills them with marrow, imparts strength. Hence to anoint, Psa 23:5.

(2) to pronounce fat. Psal. 20:4 עֹולָֽתְךָ יְדַשְּׁנֶה “pronounce thy burnt-offering fat,” i.e. accept it. (As to ־ֶה parag. comp. 1Sa 28:15.) According to Kimchi (denom. from דֶּשֶׁן compare No. 3), turn to ashes, i.e. by sending fire down from heaven, comp. 1Ki 18:24, 36 1Ki 18:36.

(3) (denom. from דֶּשֶׁן) to clear from ashes, Exo 27:3 Num 4:13.

Pual, pass. of Piel No. 1, but figuratively, to be satiated abundantly. Pro 13:4, נֶפֶשׁ חָרֻצִים תְּדֻשָּׁן “the soul of the diligent shall be abundantly filled;” Pro 28:25.

Hothpael חֻדַּשַּׁן for הֻתְדַּשַּׁן to be anointed with fatness, to be smeared, used of a sword, Isa 34:6.

The derived nouns follow immediately.

דשן

adj. [pl. דְּשֵׁנִים const. דִּשְׁנֵי]-

(1) fat, rich, [“comp. שָׁמֵן”], (used of a soil), Isa 30:23.

(2) juicy, full of sap (used of trees), Psa 92:15.

(3) rich, wealthy (used of persons), Psa 22:30. Comp. שֶׁמֶן.
